coredata.dc:title: Synthesis of Mn2+ doped ZnS quantum dots/ZIF-8 composite and its applications as a fluorescent probe for sensing Co2+ and dichromate
coredata.dc:creator: Fu, Xin
Li, Hui
Lv, Rui
Hong, Dou
Yang, Boyi
Gu, Wen
Liu, Xin
coredata.dc:description: A new type of QDs/MOF hybrid material has been successfully synthesized by a simple method, which employed Mn2+: ZnS quantum dots and ZIF-8 as original materials. The as-synthesized material exhibited excellent fluorescent property for sensing ions with an orange red emitting peak. In buffer solution, the fluorescence was sensitively quenched in the presence of Co2+, and it has also been successfully applied in the human serum albumin (HSA) buffer system with well anti-interference property. Subsequently, dichromate detection was another significant value of this fluorescent probe, and the contents of dichromate in tap water could be computed accurately by this sensor. More importantly, all the above detection progress can be monitored by naked eyes with a visual colour change from orange red to colourless under 254 nm UV-irradiation. And the possible mechanism of the detection would be discussed in detail.
coredata.dcterms:subject: ZIF-8
Mn2+:ZnS quantum dots
Composite
Co2+ and dichromate sensing
Sensing mechanism

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Preparation of Mn2+: ZnS quantum dots
Preparation of Mn2+: ZnS QDs/ZIF-8 composite
Characterization of Mn2+: ZnS QDs/ZIF-8 composite

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: The transmission electron microscope images of ZIF-8 (a-b) and Mn2+: ZnS QDs/ZIF-8 composite (c-d).
(a) XRD contrast patterns of ZIF-8 and composite. (b) IR spectra of ZIF-8 and composite.
Emission spectrum of ZIF-8, composite and Mn2+:ZnS QDs (λex = 304 nm). The inset is the corresponding picture of ZIF-8 and composite under 254 nm UV-light irradiation.

(a) Fluorescence intensity of composite at different Co2+ concentrations. (b) The Stern–Volmer plots of composite for Co2+. (c) The picture of composite containing different ions under 254 nm UV lamp irradiation.

(a) The changes of 592 nm fluorescence intensity with 200 μM several cations. (Ex = 304 nm) (b) The changes of fluorescence intensity with 200 μM several biological molecules. (Ex = 304 nm) (c) Comparison of the luminescence intensity of composite upon the addition of Co2+ in the presence of other cations. (d) Comparison of the luminescence intensity of composite upon the addition of Co2+ in the presence of other biological molecules.

(a) The emission spectra of composite at different concentrations of Cr2O7 2-. (b) The Stern–Volmer plots of composite for Cr2O7 2-. (c) The changes of 592 nm fluorescence intensity with 200 μM different anions. (d) Comparison of the luminescence intensity of composite upon the addition of Cr2O7 2- in the presence of other anions. (e) The picture of composite containing different anions under 254 nm UV lamp irradiation.
Analytical results of Co2+ detection in human serum albumin neutral buffer solution.
Analytical results of dichromate detection in tap water.

head.ce:title: Synthesis of Mn2+ doped ZnS quantum dots/ZIF-8 composite and its applications as a fluorescent probe for sensing Co2+ and dichromate

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: College of Chemistry, Key Laboratory of Advanced Energy Materials Chemistry (MOE), Tianjin Key Laboratory of Metal and Molecule Based Material Chemistry, Collaborative Innovation Center of Chemical Science and Engineering, Nankai University, Tianjin 300071, PR China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: College of Chemistry, Key Laboratory of Advanced Energy Materials Chemistry (MOE), Tianjin Key Laboratory of Metal and Molecule Based Material Chemistry, Collaborative Innovation Center of Chemical Science and Engineering, Nankai University

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: A new type of QDs/MOF hybrid material has been successfully synthesized by a simple method, which employed Mn2+: ZnS quantum dots and ZIF-8 as original materials. The as-synthesized material exhibited excellent fluorescent property for sensing ions with an orange red emitting peak. In buffer solution, the fluorescence was sensitively quenched in the presence of Co2+, and it has also been successfully applied in the human serum albumin (HSA) buffer system with well anti-interference property. Subsequently, dichromate detection was another significant value of this fluorescent probe, and the contents of dichromate in tap water could be computed accurately by this sensor. More importantly, all the above detection progress can be monitored by naked eyes with a visual colour change from orange red to colourless under 254 nm UV-irradiation. And the possible mechanism of the detection would be discussed in detail.

A new hybrid material was first proposed which combined Mn2+: ZnS quantum dots and ZIF-8. This material was regarded as a sensitive sensor for Co2+ and dichromate detection with a colour change turned from orange red to colourless. What's more, it first presented that the skeleton of MOF can be used to prevent agglomeration of quantum dots.
fx1

ce:section.ce:para: As everyone knows, metal-organic framework (MOFs) is a class of inorganic metal skeleton materials with adjustable holes and stable structures, which has attracted more attention in many important research areas. MOFs was often employed as fluorescence detector [1–4], adsorption and storage material [5–10], organic reaction catalyst [11–13] and drug carrier [14–16]. In view of these multiple advantages of MOFs materials, so far, inorganic hybrid material which combined MOFs and other nanoparticles has become a new research direction, especially in the field of sensor. Among these various hybrid materials, quantum dots are often used as object to be doped into MOFs. Specifically, ZIF-8 was a classic zeolite imidazole-based MOF with wide range of applications, resulting from its simple synthesis method and structural stability [17]. As previously reported, Biswal et al. encapsulated GODs into nano-ZIF-8 crystals which gained better photoluminescence properties [18]. Wang et al. put forward a new measurement to study oxidase activities on the basis of CdTe QDs@ZIF-8 composite [19]. Xu et al. reported CDs@ZIF-8 complex through one step synthesis method which exhibited excellent property for detecting quercetin with a low detection limit [20]. All of these literatures prove that the doping of quantum dots can make MOFs obtain better properties.

Of late, ZnS quantum dots have aroused widespread attention in virtue of its distinctive properties, such as quantum size effect, low toxicity, narrow and symmetric fluorescence emission spectra, high quantum efficiency, well water solubility and other remarkable characteristics [21]. A kind of water-soluble Mn2+ doped ZnS quantum dots materials was designed by Yan et al., which has been applied for bioanalysis successfully [22,23]. However, quantum dots may cause some agglomeration after precipitation and dry. Referring to the previous literature, ZIF-8 may be an ideal carrier of quantum dots; it can be served as a framework to ship quantum dots, resulting in reducing the agglomeration effect.

In recent years, more and more scientists focused on the detection of metal ions and anions. A numerous of measurement methods have been widely applied to sensing ions and small molecules, such as atomic absorption spectroscopy (AAS) [24,25], electrochemical (ECM) method [26,27], fluorescence method [28–30], liquid chromatography [31], etc. These methods are often expensive, time-consuming and complicated except for the fluorescence detection method.

As known to all, metallic elements play vital roles in the human body system. Cobalt (II) is essential part of the vitamin B12, it can stimulate the hematopoietic system of human bone marrow, promote the process of hemoglobin synthesis and increase the number of red blood cells [32,33]. But the requirements of cobalt (II) in the body must be in a specific range. If excessive, it may cause many diseases and other side effects, for instance, pulmonary syndrome, allergy and lung disease, neurodegenerative diseases, etc [34,35]. Hence, the accurate content detection of cobalt (II) in organisms is very necessary.

Nowadays, water environment pollution owing to industrial production has also caused much social attention. Many heavy metal ions and anions, organic pollutants and so on have become the main sources of the water environment pollution. Potassium dichromate (K2Cr2O7) is a common, toxic strong oxidant; it has been classified as the first carcinogen by the international cancer research organization [36]. But it's still an important substance in industrial production, so it's inevitable that some industrial waste would contain dichromate resulting in polluting the water environment and killing aquatic animals and plants.

Herein, we report a new binary composite material which combined Mn2+ doped ZnS quantum dots and ZIF-8. ZIF-8 could be used as a framework for loading quantum dots and the quantum dots provided excellent fluorescence properties for the composite. This as-obtained material exhibited excellent property for sensing cobalt (II) and dichromate, which has low detection limit and visual detection progress from orange red to colourless observed by naked eyes under 254 nm UV-irradiation.

All chemicals were purchased from merchants and with no further purification, they were all analytical grade. The water used in all experiments and aqueous solutions was ultrapure water (18.2 MΩ), which was made by water purification system. Zinc vitriol (ZnSO4·7H2O), (CH3COO)2Mn, cysteine (Cys), Na2S·9H2O, NaOH, 2-methylimidazole, methanol and Zn(NO3)2·6H2O were used to synthetize composite. All salts used in cationic detection were nitrates. Na2SO4, Na2SO3, NaNO2, KCl, KBr, KI, KClO3, KBrO3, K2C2O4, KHCO3, CH3COOK, K2CO3, K2Cr2O7 were used for anionic detection. The biomolecules and reagents used were listed as follow: fructose, glutose, galactose, valine (Val), histidine (His), lysine (Lys), asparagic acid (Asp), human serum albumin (HSA).

The TEM and HR-TEM pictures were taken by FEI Tecnai G2 S-Twin transmission electron microscope. And the field emission scanning electron microscope (JEOL JSM-7500F) was used to taking the surface photos of samples and element distribution. The data of X-ray powder diffraction were collected on D8 Focus diffractometer with Cu-Kα radiation (λ = 0.154 nm). The FT-IR spectra of the composite were measured on Bruker Tensor 27 FTIR spectrometer using KBr pellet. The BET surface areas measured by nitrogen adsorption using Micromeritics Tristar II 3020 apparatus. All fluorescence properties were tested on FLS 920P Edinburgh apparatus. In order to explain mechanisms, SHIMADZU UV-3600 spectrometer was used for UV–vis spectra analysis. ICP analysis was performed on ICP-OES (SPECTRO-BLUE). The XPS spectra were measured on X-ray Photoelectron Spectrometer (Axis Ultra DLD).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Preparation of Mn2+: ZnS quantum dots
ce:section.ce:para: The quantum dots were synthesized according to the Yan's work [22]. The manganese source was replaced by Mn(Ac)2. The main difference was that Mn2+:ZnS QDs were dispersed into methanol after centrifugation and washing. (20 mL methanol containing 2 mmol ZnS QDs)

ce:section.ce:section-title: Preparation of Mn2+: ZnS QDs/ZIF-8 composite
ce:section.ce:para: On the basis of the literature [17], Zn(NO3)2·6H2O methanol solution and 2-methylimidazole methanol solution were prepared, respectively. Then, 20 mL Mn2+: ZnS QDs methanol dispersion were poured into Zn(NO3)2·6H2O, the solution of 2-methylimidazole was added subsequently, and stirred 24 h at room temperature. Then the composite was collected by centrifugation (8000 r/min) and washed with methanol. The collected solid were dried in drying oven set at 50 °C.

The solid of composite was through simple drying and fine grinding before detection. PBS buffer solution (pH = 7.2) was used for sensing, the composite was soaked into it (2 mg/5 mL) and then sonicated for 10 min. Typically, various aqueous solutions (1.0 × 10−2 M) of cations, anions or amino acids and sugars were prepared by ultra-water for further use. The conditions of excitation slit and emission slit were set as 4 nm and 3 nm under 304 nm exactions in whole fluorescence experiments.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Characterization of Mn2+: ZnS QDs/ZIF-8 composite
ce:section.ce:para: Firstly, the morphology of the quantum dots and composite was measured by transmission electron microscope (TEM). Fig. S1 showed the morphology of the newly prepared Mn2+: ZnS QDs aqueous solution, which were nearly spherical in shape and the size were about 3 nm in uniform. But a certain agglomeration occurred after drying into solid. (Fig. S2) There was no great change to observe the comparison morphology pictures of ZIF-8 and composite. As shown in Fig. 1c-d, the shape of composite was hexagonal shape which was as same as ZIF-8 (Fig. 1a-b), and the size were about 60 nm. As shown in Fig. 2a, the framework of ZIF-8 didn’t collapse after loading Mn2+: ZnS QDs, which can be proved by XRD patterns. In FT-IR spectra (Fig. 2b), the basic skeleton of composite still existed after loading nanoparticles and the major groups could be easily found. The infrared spectra showed peaks at 1421, 1384, 1309, 995 cm−1, which were assigned to vibrations of C-C stretch, C-H rock, C-N stretch, =C-H bend, respectively. The N2 adsorption–desorption isotherms of ZIF-8 and composite were shown in Fig. S3. Typical Type-I isotherms were obtained which demonstrated that they were microporous materials. The BET surface of ZIF-8 decreased from 933.883 m2 g−1 to 764.605 m2 g−1, suggested that the quantum dots were shipped into ZIF-8. In order to certify the binding energy of the elements in composites, XPS analysis was also carried out. As shown in Fig. S4a-e, the C1s signals located at 284.62 eV and 285.8 eV and the two peaks around suggested 399 eV for N1s were attributed to the imidazole ligand in ZIF-8 [37,38]. In the Zn2p spectra, two peaks appeared at 1021.2 eV (2P3/2) and 1044.2 eV (2P1/2), proving that the existence of the zinc (II) in the composite [39]. And the two peaks at 161.3 eV and 162.8 eV of S2p were assigned to ZnS [40–42]. Otherwise, ZIF-8 was combined with Mn2+:ZnS QDs, so the composite may be a promising luminescent material. As shown in Fig. 3, the fluorescence spectra of composite exhibited the same emissions as Mn2+:ZnS QDs located at 592 nm, which was owing to the 4T1→6A1 transition process of the Mn2+ according to literature [22]. While the ZIF-8 has no fluorescence emission under same exaction, it can be easily observed the colour under 254 nm UV-light irradiation. (The inset picture in Fig. 3) The fluorescence comparison of solid quantum dots and composite was also investigated. The fluorescence intensity of quantum dots decreased a lot after drying, but the composite was not affected (Fig. S5). On account of the previous TEM pictures, it can be concluded that ZIF-8 can be used as a framework for loading quantum dots to avoid certain agglomeration. In order to measure the content of Mn2+ and ZnS in composite, we performed ICP analysis to obtain data. As shown in Table S1, the content of Mn2+ in composite was 1.09 ppm = 1.09 mg/kg = 0.02 mol/kg. The ratio of initial theory concentration of the Mn and Zn was Mn:Zn = 0.03:1, so we can calculate the content of ZnS quantum dots in the composite based on above element ratio and manganese content. So the content of ZnS quantum dots was about 0.667 mol/kg.

A serious of fluorescence experiment was carried to research the nature of metal ions recognition. Several common ions were added into the composite solution, such as Na+, K+, Mg2+, Al3+, Sr2+, Pb2+, Cr3+, Cd2+, NH4 +, Fe3+, Zn2+, Fe2+, Cu2+ and Co2+. After the addition of an equal amount of cation solutions, most of these ions didn’t significantly affect the fluorescence intensity of composite except for Fe3+, Fe2+, Cu2+ and Co2+ (Fig. 5a). Both Fe3+ and Fe2+ quenched about 40% of fluorescence intensity, and Cu2+ quenched about 60% of fluorescence intensity, while the addition of Co2+ almost completely quenched the fluorescence intensity. The fluorescence decreased gradually with the adding of Co2+ ions from 0 to 100 μM ( Fig. 4a), and the relationship of I0/I-1 and concentration of Co2+ was shown in Fig. 4b. The quenching constant was calculated, the Ksv of Co2+ detection was 4.311 × 104 L/mol. On the basis of the ratio of 3σ/k, the detection limit of Co2+ was 0.27 μM, which was better than the other methods stated in the literature (Table S2). The quenching progress could be observed by naked eyes under ultraviolet light, which indicated that the colour of the composite solution would turn colourless in the presence of Co2+ (Fig. 4c). On the other hand, the value of I/I0 can reflect the quenching degrees more directly compared with other ions, it was noted that Co2+ almost completely quenched the fluorescence intensity ( Fig. 5a). In addition, fluorescence showed a stable trend under other biological small molecules environment (Fig. 5b), and the detection was not interfered by other metal ions and small biological molecules (Fig. 5c-d).

To explore the potential mechanism of the above detection, many testing ways were carried out such as SEM, UV–vis, XPS, XRD, etc. UV–vis spectra was first used to consider whether the electronic absorption interaction or not (Fig. S6). The absorption spectra didn’t have overlap with the excitation spectrum or emission spectrum of the composite. Therefore, the mechanism was not electron transfer or absorption process. As shown in Fig. S7, the SEM mapping pictures of composite which was filtered after the detection of Co2+ had a residue onto the surface of composite. It suggested that the ions would have certain effects between the analyte and composite. The mechanism of detection could be explained more directly by XRD patterns (Fig. S8). The skeleton of MOF did not collapse except for a few stray peaks. The contrast pattern of ZIF-8 also proved that MOF had not much interaction with Co2+ ions. In order to explore the substitution of cation ions during the detection, we measured the concentration of Mn2+ and Zn2+ in composite before and after the Co2+ detection by ICP analysis. The results were shown in Table S1, the ratio of Mn2+ and Zn2+ in composite was about 0.02, which was similar to Co2+-composite system. It can be indicated that Mn2+ and Zn2+ would not be substituted by Co2+ during the detection. What's more, the framework of ZIF-8 not only can prevent quantum dots from accumulation, but it also can selectively accumulate target analyte due to the adsorption property resulting in fluorescence quenching [43]. The ICP analysis also proved this conclusion which showed that some Co2+ ions existed after the detection. XPS spectra were used to further confirm the mechanism by analysing binding energy of every element. The electron bonding energy of C1s, N1s, O1s, Zn2p spectra has almost no change after the detection, it also deduced the skeleton structure of ZIF-8 had not been damaged (Fig. S9a-d). In Co2p spectra (Fig. S9f), the four peaks located at 781 eV, 786.3 eV, 796.7 eV, 802.5 eV was probably attributed to the characteristic bands of cobaltous oxide (Co2+) which had two characteristic satellite peaks [44–48]. The S2p spectra of Co2+ system had the same peaks as the composite (Fig. S9e). Some literatures have been reported that the metal ions would have interaction with –SH or –COOH [49,50], so the fluorescence quenching may due to the bonds between Co2+ and functional groups onto the Mn2+: ZnS QDs.

In a typical experiment, 14 anions solution were prepared to study the property of sensing anions in water, such as Br-, Cl-, I-, CO3 2-, HCO3 -, SO4 2-, SO3 2-, NO3 -, NO2 -, C2O4 2-, OAc-, BrO3 -, ClO3 -, Cr2O7 2-. As depicted in Fig. 6a, the fluorescence intensity of 592 nm was gradually quenched with the addition of dichromate. The value of the Stern-Volmer constants (Ksv) was calculated on the equation: I0/I-1 = Ksv[C (Cr2O7 2-)], which was 2.742 × 104 L/mol (Fig. 6b). The detection limit based on 3σ/k was 0.22 μM, which had a great advantage compared with other quantum dots and MOF materials (Table S3). Apparently, this LOD was lower than the standard of dichromate in drinking water (0.96 µM) according to the WHO guidelines, and the response time was also in several seconds. The degree of luminescence change based on the value of I/I0 of all anions were showed in Fig. 6c, dichromate may quenched almost 80% fluorescence intensity compared with other ions. To study the anti-interference ability of the composite for sensing dichromate, we also carried the experiment in the presence of other anions which showed the similar results (Fig. 6d). The colour changes of composite compared with other anions were shown in Fig. 6e.

The absorption spectra of these anions were measured in order to figure out the mechanism. As shown in Fig. S10, most anions had absorption peaks in the range of 190–250 nm in addition to dichromate. The peaks of dichromate were located at 200–400 nm which have an overlap with the excitation spectra of the composite (304 nm). So it may have certain energy transfer process between dichromate and composite on the basis of FRET mechanism which can quench the fluorescence of the composite [51].

In order to confirm the practicability of the above conclusions, actual samples were used to quantify the content of ions. All the concentrations of samples were calculated on the previous study results, the following Eq. (1) was for Co2+, and Eq. (2) was for dichromate:
(1)
I
0
/ I - 1 = - 0 .02846 + 0 .04311 *
[
C
cobalt
]
;
(2)
I
0
/ I - 1 = 0 .06639 + 0 .02742 *
[
C
dichromate
]

It is well known that the sensing of metal ions in human body is especially important in real life. Thus, human serum albumin (HSA) neutral buffer solution was selected to confirm the detection of Co2+. As shown in Table 1, the amounts of Co2+ were consists with the added contents and the recoveries were all around 100% ± 5%. The relative standard deviation (RSD) was also within acceptable range. Similarly, same method was used for the determination of dichromate. We selected tap water as real sample with the purpose of monitoring the content of dichromate pollutant in water system. As the Table 2 presented, the value of recoveries all were within reasonable limits and the relative standard deviation (RSD) were all within 3%. The composite was a promising efficient sensor for detecting Co2+ in biological system and dichromate in water environment.

In this study, a new fluorescence probe was synthesized though a handy method. This composite can be served as a sensor for the detection of Co2+ and dichromate. The detection limit of these ions was 0.27 μM and 0.22 μM, respectively. The progress of experiment also can be observed by naked eyes with a colour change from orange red to colourless under 254 nm UV-lamp irradiation. Besides, the probe also can be applied into real samples, such as human serum albumin neutral buffer solution and tap water. Therefore, the proposed composite may be a promising multifunctional probe in monitoring analysis.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work was supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China (21371103) and 111 project (B12015).

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data associated with this article can be found in the online version at doi:10.1016/j.jssc.2018.04.021.
